raw_consumer_statement: In XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX, I requested an additional XXXX XXXX card for my husband 's use. In early XXXX, my son contacted me about a package that he was expecting and I informed him that I had not received it yet. After tracking the package online, I discovered that it had been stolen from my mailbox. I reached out to the individual whom this item was shipped from to let them know what had occurred. They agreed to replace it as long as I would make an additional purchase. I have this conversation saved. What I did not realize at the time was not only had the package been stolen but the XXXX card that I had requested was also stolen. On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a text message from XXXX XXXX  Citi Fraud asking me if I was attempting a purchase of {$320.00} at a store location in XXXXXXXX XXXX. I replied " No '' and then logged into my XXXX account immediately after the text. That was when I discovered that there had been multiple purchases on my husband 's stolen XXXX card. The first charge of {$250.00} occurred on XX/XX/XXXX followed by two additional charges of {$110.00} and {$270.00} both on XX/XX/XXXX. All of these charges occurred in the XXXX area and I do not live anywhere close to XXXX. It appears that even after I had replied to the text that the {$320.00} was fraudulent, XXXX XXXX Citi still expects me to pay for the charge. I contacted their fraud department right away and the representative that I spoke with was very difficult for me to understand. I did understand that she kept trying to tell me that my account was opened fraudulently. I repeatedly told her that was not the case and the charges on my card were due to a stolen card. After telling her numerous times that the account activity was from a stolen card, she submitted the case as a fraudulently opened account. I did not realize this at the time. I only found out about that once I received their decision on my case in a letter. It stated that due to my remitted payments from a valid account and receiving benefits from the disputed charges I am responsible for the fraudulent charges! I will gladly pay for what I am responsible for but I will not pay for fraudulent charges totaling {$970.00}. Once I read the letter, I called them once again. As soon as the rep was on the line and I could not understand her due to her thick XXXX  I requested a supervisor that I could understand. I spoke with XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX and he seemed to understand exactly what the issue was and proclaimed that he would help me. It is now XX/XX/XXXX. I have not heard back from anyone and the interest charges keep growing on the unpaid balance on my XXXX XXXX
